scope: Full statutory audit of IDC consolidated financial statements for FY2024 including all subsidiary entities.

findings.title: Subsidiary Governance Documentation Gaps
findings.description: Board minutes and governance records for three subsidiary entities were incomplete or not maintained in accordance with IDC governance policy.
findings.recommendation: Strengthen subsidiary board reporting requirements and implement centralised governance document repository.
findings.managementResponse: Accepted. Centralised document repository being implemented. Completion target Q2 2025.
findings.actionTaken: Governance policy circulated to all subsidiary company secretaries.

findings.title: Investment Valuation Methodology
findings.description: Investment valuation policy does not fully align with IFRS 13 fair value hierarchy requirements for Level 3 unquoted investments.
findings.recommendation: Update investment valuation policy to align with IFRS 13; implement quarterly portfolio valuation reviews.
findings.managementResponse: Accepted. Updated valuation policy drafted and under review by Audit Committee.

findings.title: Related-Party Transaction Disclosures
findings.description: Related-party transaction register not consistently maintained; two transactions not disclosed in the notes to financial statements.
findings.recommendation: Improve related-party transaction register maintenance; implement quarterly review process.
findings.managementResponse: Accepted. Register updated and quarterly review process implemented.
findings.actionTaken: Register updated. Quarterly review process implemented and first review completed.

executiveSummary: Unqualified audit opinion issued for FY2024. Three management letter points raised relating to subsidiary governance documentation, investment valuation methodology, and related-party transaction disclosures.

scope: Q3 2024 internal audit covering related-party transactions, investment monitoring, and board secretariat processes across IDC and portfolio companies.

findings.title: Related-Party Transaction Approval Process
findings.description: Related-party transaction approval process not consistently followed in two subsidiaries — transactions approved without full board sign-off.
findings.recommendation: Enforce related-party transaction policy across all subsidiaries; implement mandatory pre-approval checklist.
findings.managementResponse: Accepted. Policy enforcement memo issued to all subsidiary CEOs.
findings.actionTaken: Policy enforcement memo issued. Pre-approval checklist implemented in both subsidiaries.

findings.title: Investment Monitoring Report Timeliness
findings.description: Investment monitoring reports not submitted on time by three portfolio companies — average delay of 12 days beyond required submission date.
findings.recommendation: Implement automated monitoring report reminder system; escalate persistent non-compliance to IDC board.
findings.actionTaken: Automated reminder system implemented. All three portfolio companies now submitting on time.

findings.title: Board Minutes Circulation Delay
findings.description: Board minutes not circulated within required 5-day period in one subsidiary — average delay of 9 days.
findings.recommendation: Strengthen board secretariat processes in subsidiaries; implement minutes circulation tracking.
findings.actionTaken: Minutes circulation tracking implemented. Subsidiary company secretary retrained.

executiveSummary: Three medium-risk findings identified in Q3 2024 internal audit. All findings accepted by management with remediation actions assigned.

scope: Full statutory audit of ZESCO Limited financial statements for FY2024.

findings.title: Going Concern — Below-Cost-Recovery Tariffs
findings.description: ZESCO tariffs remain below cost-recovery levels, creating dependency on GRZ subsidies. Emphasis of matter paragraph included in audit opinion.
findings.recommendation: Progress tariff application to Energy Regulation Board; develop medium-term financial sustainability plan.
findings.managementResponse: Tariff application submitted to ERB in February 2025. Decision expected Q3 2025.

findings.title: Procurement Controls
findings.description: Procurement committee oversight gaps identified — four contracts awarded without full committee approval documentation.
findings.recommendation: Strengthen procurement committee oversight; implement mandatory approval documentation checklist.
findings.managementResponse: Accepted. Procurement policy updated and committee processes strengthened.
findings.actionTaken: Procurement policy updated. All committee members retrained.

findings.title: IT General Controls
findings.description: IT access controls review overdue — privileged user access not reviewed in 18 months.
findings.recommendation: Implement IT access controls review; establish semi-annual privileged access review cycle.
findings.actionTaken: Full IT access review completed. Semi-annual review cycle established.

executiveSummary: Unqualified opinion with emphasis of matter on going concern related to below-cost-recovery tariffs and GRZ subsidy dependency. Four management letter points on procurement controls and IT general controls.

scope: Full statutory audit of Zambia Railways Limited financial statements for FY2024.

findings.title: Fixed Asset Register — Track Infrastructure
findings.description: Qualified opinion issued due to inability to verify completeness of fixed asset register for track infrastructure. Estimated unverified assets of ZMW 2.3 billion.
findings.recommendation: Commission independent fixed asset verification exercise for all track infrastructure.
findings.managementResponse: Accepted. Independent fixed asset verification commissioned.
findings.actionTaken: Independent verification firm appointed. Field work commenced.

findings.title: Fleet Maintenance Records
findings.description: Maintenance records for 34% of locomotive fleet incomplete or missing, creating safety and insurance compliance risks.
findings.recommendation: Implement fleet management system; reconstruct missing maintenance records where possible.
findings.managementResponse: Fleet management system procurement in progress.

findings.title: Safety Compliance Documentation
findings.description: Safety compliance documentation for 12 stations not current — certificates expired or not renewed.
findings.recommendation: Strengthen safety compliance documentation; implement renewal tracking system.
findings.actionTaken: Renewal applications submitted for all 12 stations.

executiveSummary: Qualified opinion due to inability to verify completeness of fixed asset register for track infrastructure. Going concern emphasis of matter. Six management letter points on revenue recognition, fleet maintenance records, and safety compliance documentation.

scope: Full statutory audit of ZCCM-IH consolidated financial statements for FY2024.

findings.title: Mine Rehabilitation Fund Adequacy
findings.description: Preliminary findings indicate emphasis of matter paragraph likely on mine rehabilitation fund adequacy — actuarial review suggests ZMW 570 million shortfall.
findings.recommendation: Commission independent actuarial review; develop funding plan to address shortfall.
findings.managementResponse: Management engaging with auditors on rehabilitation fund valuation methodology.

executiveSummary: Audit in progress. Preliminary findings indicate emphasis of matter paragraph likely on mine rehabilitation fund adequacy.
